meta.note: March 17 evening run — LucidLink Connect full detail (Emmy award, 90 PB, no egress fees), Backlight vertical publishing +120% confirmed, long-form counter-trend signal (Metricool), agentic AI vertical video pipelines, iconik UI navigation overhaul coming March

decisionPanels.title: 🚨 Iran Conflict Day 4 — MMR endurance test entering extended phase
decisionPanels.summary: Guardian is now into Day 4 of sustained US-Iran conflict publishing. This is no longer a sprint; it is a sustained operational load test for MMR's architecture. The workflow under stress right now is the single best data set available before go-live: LucidLink ingest under sustained use, iconik collection management at editorial pace, Wildmoka distribution velocity on a multi-day story. The question has shifted from 'can the system publish fast?' to 'can it maintain quality output across 96+ hours without workflow degradation?' Any friction discovered now is infinitely cheaper to fix before go-live than after.
actions: DEBRIEF (highest priority): Schedule a structured producer debrief once the acute publishing phase eases. Ask: what broke, what was slow, what worked. This is the most valuable data available before MMR go-live.
DOCUMENT: Log workflow bottlenecks as they appear. These become the opening section of the MMR risk register and inform handover documentation.
DO NOT push new tooling changes during the hot phase. Stability of workflow under load is more valuable than any new feature right now.

decisionPanels.title: ☁️ LucidLink Connect — Emmy-winning platform, no egress fees, Guardian archive activation
decisionPanels.summary: LucidLink Connect (GA: March 5) is now fully documented. New detail tonight: LucidLink manages 90+ petabytes for 100,000+ users globally. It won an Emmy from the Academy of Television Arts & Sciences Engineering, Science & Technology category in 2025. Critically: LucidLink does not charge egress fees — in a world of rising cloud storage costs (storage cost is a central finding of Backlight's 2026 report), this is a meaningful differentiator. Connect enables byte-range streaming of existing S3-compatible stores without data migration. CEO quote: 'Customers told us they got 10 PB or 100 PB sitting in buckets that they want to activate.' If The Guardian has S3 archives, Connect makes them immediately usable without touching or copying the data.
actions: ASK (infrastructure/cloud team): Does Guardian have existing media archives in AWS S3 or S3-compatible storage? If yes, LucidLink Connect (Enterprise tier) activates them without migration — the economics are direct.

CONFIRM: Which version of LucidLink is Guardian's WIP filespace running? If Classic, the March 2 Upgrade Tool enables in-place upgrade to unlock Connect, Guest Access, and Directory Management API — all without data copying.
NOTE: Azure Blob storage support is on the LucidLink Connect roadmap. If Guardian uses Azure, this is an upcoming unlock worth tracking.

decisionPanels.title: 📊 Backlight 2026: Vertical +120%, Audio +45%, Video 64% of Storage — The Guardian MMR numbers
decisionPanels.summary: The Backlight 2026 Iconik Media Stats Report (published March 10, now widely covered) contains the industry-wide data that validates every key MMR architectural decision. Vertical publishing grew 120% — confirming 9:16 as a first-class production format, not a conversion afterthought. Audio assets grew 45% year-over-year, driven by podcasts, multi-language tracks, and voice-overs. Video is 64% of storage despite being only 14% of asset count — validating LucidLink's storage architecture over flat object storage. AI was used 11.4 million times in 2025 across Backlight's product suite. These are not forecasts — they are 903 million assets across 324 petabytes of confirmed production data. This report is the single most credible data source for making the business case for Guardian's MMR investment.
actions: USE THIS REPORT: The 64% storage / 14% asset count ratio is the most powerful statistic for making the business case to Guardian infrastructure/finance. Video is the most expensive asset class by far.
AUDIT: Check iconik metadata schema for audio fields — podcast episode/series, language, transcript attachment. Audio +45% YoY means Guardian's audio needs will compound fast if schema isn't ready.
ENSURE Project Launcher covers audio collection creation workflows from day one — not as a follow-on. Backlight data shows audio is growing faster than video on a percentage basis.

decisionPanels.title: 🏭 NAB 2026 (April 18-22): 32 days — Backlight, Harmonic, Avid, EditShare, AWS all confirmed
decisionPanels.summary: The NAB 2026 vendor picture now includes AWS Elemental Inference (W1701) alongside Backlight (N2829), Harmonic Spectrum X Plus (W2831), Avid AI, and EditShare FLOW AI + MediaSilo AI (N1251). Five directly relevant vendors in one room. AWS Elemental Inference is the most significant new addition: it applies AI during encoding (not after) to produce 9:16 vertical clips at 6-10 second latency from live feeds — Fox Sports cut turnaround from 45-60 minutes to under 15 minutes. This is the technology that enables Guardian-scale live breaking news vertical publishing. Seeing it demoed at NAB with a Guardian use case framing in mind would be high value.
actions: PLAN: Decide NAB 2026 attendance before April 1. Five key vendors in one room in 32 days. This is the best possible use of two days' travel for MMR context.
PREP: Build vendor question list: iconik Analytical AI improvements, audio workflow schema, LucidLink Connect + Frame.io integration status, AWS Elemental Inference pricing model for news publishers.
AWS Elemental Inference pitch: 'AI during encoding, not after.' That 6-10s latency vs 45-60min means the Guardian could publish vertical clips of breaking events while they are still breaking.

sections.title: Vendor Watch: iconik / LucidLink / Backlight / Wildmoka

items.title: iconik March 2026: redesigned navigation, neutral grey background, admin dropdown restructured — UI overhaul rolling out; update producer training materials before MMR go-live
iconik changelog March 5, 2026: My Shares filtering + favourite saved searches — confirmed no new changelog items March 6-17
Backlight 2026 Iconik Media Stats Report — video 64% of storage, audio +45% YoY, vertical +120%, AI used 11.4M times, 903M assets / 324 PB total
LucidLink Connect GA (March 5): Emmy-winning platform (TV Arts Academy 2025), 90+ PB managed, 100,000+ users, no egress fees — mount S3 buckets as Filespaces without data migration; Azure Blob coming
LucidLink Upgrade Tool (March 2): upgrade Classic Filespaces to new LucidLink in-place — no data copying; unlocks Connect, Guest Access, Directory Management API
Wildmoka supports publishing to social drafts (Facebook/Instagram drafts, YouTube unlisted) — batch social scheduling workflow available now for Guardian social teams without live-publish risk
NAB Show 2026 (April 18-22): Backlight (N2829), Harmonic Spectrum X Plus (W2831), AWS Elemental Inference (W1701), Avid AI, EditShare FLOW AI + MediaSilo AI (N1251)
Guardian Iran Day 4 (March 17): sustained conflict publishing entering extended phase — MMR endurance test; debrief window approaching
Guardian SPUR coalition: BBC, FT, Sky News, Telegraph building AI content standards — UK AI copyright delay strengthens early-mover position; MMR schema should incorporate rights/licensing fields
Guardian Studios: Leah Green (ex-Channel 4) as head — personality-led journalism, video-first mandate; Project Launcher must support journalist-as-creator social publishing workflows
Guardian US 30M audience (+21% YoY, Nov 2025 Comscore): highest US traffic of any UK newspaper brand — US audience is the growth engine; YouTube is the distribution layer

INMA March 2026: YouTube CEO 'Creators are the new studios' — publishers competing against self-funded creators with lower costs + better audience data; YouTube UK's #2 streaming service (behind BBC, ahead of ITV)
DCN March 12: publishers now actively hiring YouTube-specialist video producers — YouTube climbing priority lists in 2026 as AI cuts search referral traffic by up to 79%
Reuters Institute 2026: YouTube main publisher focus (+74 net score); TikTok +56, Instagram +41; publishers deprioritising Facebook (-23) and X (-52); search traffic -40% over 3 years
TikTok Bulletin Boards rolling out to publishers/brands in For You feed (March 13) — direct audience notification tool; Guardian should test alongside YouTube live control room vertical
YouTube March 13: live stream → instant vertical layout in live control room — zero-cost Shorts from any live feed, zero engineering required

Counter-trend signal (Metricool 2026 Social Study): long-form video and authentic storytelling returning — audiences seeking 'more human rhythm' after 5 years of short-form AI content saturation; mix formats intentionally

AWS Elemental Inference (GA): AI during encoding (not after) — 9:16 vertical at 6-10s latency from live feeds; Fox Sports cut turnaround from 45-60 min to <15 min; Fox + NBCUniversal live; demoing at NAB W1701
Agentic AI video editing pipelines 2026: automatic reframing 16:9→9:16, subject tracking, caption styling for silent viewing — scalable platform-native output from single source video
EditShare FLOW AI + MediaSilo AI at NAB 2026 (N1251): Analytical AI for auto-metadata (speech, faces, scenes, text) — new MediaSilo bundles with unlimited AI and forensic watermarking

INMA: YouTube CEO says AI is now 'a substrate not a feature' — 20M users using Ask feature; AI Shorts generation; autodubbing at scale. Publishers treating AI as assistive tool risk falling behind audience expectations
Reuters Institute 2026: 'liquid content' + AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) + search -40% — MMR iconik metadata schema must support format-agnostic liquid content packaging from creation
Harmonic Spectrum X Plus for NAB 2026: next-gen media server — simplified ingest/playout, lower cost per channel, new AI-driven workflow innovations (W2831)

changes.summary: March 17 run: Iran conflict enters Day 4 — debrief window approaching. LucidLink Connect deepened with Emmy award, 90+ PB scale, no egress fees, and Azure Blob roadmap — elevates strategic case significantly. Backlight's vertical +120% confirmed as core business case data. New tonight: agentic AI video editing pipelines (automated 9:16 reframing/subject tracking at scale) and a counter-trend signal from Metricool 2026 study — long-form + authentic storytelling is returning after short-form saturation. NAB 2026 now 32 days away. No new iconik changelog since March 5. iconik UI overhaul (navigation + grey background) confirmed rolling out March — update producer training materials.
